assertions.description: User says 'tech' — skill maps to 'Information Technology' exactly
assertions.check: tool calls use sectors: ['Information Technology'] — not 'tech' or 'Technology'

assertions.description: Output is inline HTML — no markdown
assertions.check: response contains <style> and HTML elements; zero markdown table syntax (|---|)
response contains section headers for: Executive Summary, Sector Movers, Sentiment & Investment, Trending Topics, Event Analysis, Company Rankings, Executive & Analyst Voice, Risk Themes
response contains phrase 'Sector sentiment is RISING' or 'Sector sentiment is FALLING' with a numeric score
cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js appears exactly one time in response
response contains 9 Chart.js canvas elements with unique IDs

assertions.description: Batch 1 fires all three calls simultaneously
assertions.check: getTopMovers, getTrends, and getAnalytics are all called before any Batch 2 calls begin
getTrends call does not include a query or topicSearchQuery field
Section 7 contains 'Most bullish executive:' with a name, role, company, and score
Section 2 contains a Potential Buy Signals card with companies from underperforming[investmentScore]

assertions.description: No tool names in visible report text
assertions.check: response does not contain: getAnalytics, getSpeakers, getTopMovers, getTrends, searchTopCompanies, getSpeakerCompanies — in visible report text (OK in comments or JS)

assertions.description: Company links use correct ProntoNLP URL format
assertions.check: anchor hrefs match pattern: {org}.prontonlp.com/#/ref/$COMPANY{id}

name: Movers Report — performance-focused query
input: Top movers in financials this month

assertions.description: Mode is Movers Report — only Sections 1–2 rendered
assertions.check: response contains Executive Summary and Sector Movers sections; does NOT contain Trending Topics or Voice of the Market sections
sinceDay passed to tools is approximately 30 days before today
tool calls use sectors: ['Financials']
response contains multiple leaderboard cards for investment score, sentiment, and stock change
response contains <style> block and HTML elements; no markdown

name: Theme Analysis — topic-focused query
input: What are semiconductor companies talking about in earnings calls?

assertions.check: tool calls use sectors: ['Information Technology-Semiconductors and Semiconductor Equipment']

assertions.description: Mode is Theme Analysis — Sections 1, 4–6 rendered
assertions.check: response contains Trending Topics, Event Analysis, Company Rankings — does NOT contain Sector Movers leaderboards or Speaker Voice section

assertions.description: Trending topics table present with at least 15 rows
assertions.check: Section 4 contains a table with at least 15 topic rows

assertions.description: Fastest-rising and fastest-declining topics called out
assertions.check: response identifies top 3 fastest-rising themes and top 2 fastest-declining

assertions.description: Company rankings by top theme present
assertions.check: Section 6 shows company ranking for at least one topic with sentiment scores and mention counts

name: Skill should NOT trigger — single company

assertions.check: pronto-company-intelligence skill is invoked for JPMorgan; no sector leaderboard or trending topics table in response

assertions.description: Response is a single-company intelligence report
assertions.check: response is a standard company intelligence HTML report for JPMorgan — no sector movers section

name: Sentiment Report — sentiment-focused query
input: How bullish is the energy sector right now?

assertions.description: Mode is Sentiment Report — Sections 1, 3, 6–7 rendered
assertions.check: response contains Sentiment & Investment Scores and Speaker Voice sections; does NOT contain full trending topic table or event analysis
tool calls use sectors: ['Energy']

assertions.description: Sector sentiment direction explicitly stated with score
assertions.check: response contains 'RISING' or 'FALLING' and a numeric sentiment score for the Energy sector

assertions.description: Executive and analyst voice sections present
assertions.check: Section 7 present with most bullish exec and most bearish analyst identified
response contains <style> and HTML structure; no markdown tables
